concepts.name: Action potential
concepts.description: The all-or-none electrical impulse that travels along a neuron.

concepts.name: Resting membrane potential
concepts.description: The voltage across a neuron's membrane at rest, near -70 mV.

concepts.name: Synaptic transmission
concepts.description: How a signal crosses the synapse from one neuron to the next.

body.prompt: Which ion influx drives the rising phase of an action potential?
body.explanation: Voltage-gated Na+ channels open first; Na+ rushes in down its electrochemical gradient, depolarizing the membrane. K+ efflux comes later and drives repolarization.

body.prompt: The Na+/K+ pump maintains the resting potential by moving ions in which ratio?
body.explanation: The pump exports 3 Na+ for every 2 K+ it imports, using ATP. The net export of positive charge helps keep the inside negative relative to the outside.

body.prompt: Arrival of an action potential at the axon terminal triggers neurotransmitter release by opening which channels?
body.explanation: Depolarization opens voltage-gated Ca2+ channels; the Ca2+ influx makes synaptic vesicles fuse with the presynaptic membrane and release their neurotransmitter into the cleft.
